summary: PRC field standardization testing - verified total_redeemed calculation across all redemption collections uses centralized get_prc_amount() helper correctly

issues.description: PRC statement endpoint fails with datetime comparison error - '<' not supported between instances of 'datetime.datetime' and 'str'
issues.affected_test: Not tested - separate issue from PRC field standardization

action_items: Fix PRC statement endpoint datetime comparison bug (separate from PRC field standardization)
context_for_next_testing_agent: PRC field standardization is working correctly. The get_prc_amount() helper in /app/backend/utils/prc_fields.py correctly reads PRC amounts from various legacy field names (prc_used, prc_deducted, prc_amount, total_prc). The /api/user/{user_id}/redeem-limit endpoint returns accurate total_redeemed values. The /api/admin/members/list endpoint correctly shows total_redeemed in the redeem_limit object for each member. Test user 6c96a6cc-08a2-442c-8e2d-f1fb6f18aa21 shows total_redeemed of ~20500 PRC which is calculated correctly across all redemption collections.
key_features_verified: get_prc_amount() helper reads PRC from multiple field names in priority order
User redeem limit API returns accurate total_redeemed
Admin members list shows correct total_redeemed for each member
Elite/Growth/Explorer subscription plans have correct base limits
Remaining limit calculation (total_limit - total_redeemed) is accurate
